title: podman system df triggers panic
body:
### Issue Description

running `podman system df` produce the following output. The exit code is 2
```
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x0 pc=0xf3cb02]

goroutine 1 [running]:
github.com/containers/common/libimage.(*Runtime).DiskUsage(0xc0001c9000, {0x1bc75c8, 0xc0003ab4d0})
        github.com/containers/common/libimage/disk_usage.go:68 +0x542
github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi.(*ContainerEngine).SystemDf(0xc0004443f8, {0x1bc75c8?, 0xc0003ab4d0?}, {{0xbe3a0d2a00000000?, 0xc000423ac0?}, 0x0?})
        github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/infra/abi/system.go:261 +0x47
github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/system.df(0x26d3d60, {0x27ff280?, 0x0?, 0x0?})
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/system/df.go:55 +0x6d
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).execute(0x26d3d60, {0xc000040410, 0x0, 0x0})
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:983 +0xaca
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteC(0x26bd040)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1115 +0x3ff
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).Execute(...)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1039
github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteContext(...)
        github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:1032
main.Execute()
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/root.go:115 +0xb4
main.main()
        github.com/containers/podman/cmd/podman/main.go:60 +0x452
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a Ubuntu 24.04 WSL2 instance
2. Run `apt-get install podman`
3. Run `podman system df`


### Describe the results you received

Posted in `issue description` section

### Describe the results you expected

Do not panic

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I'm running podman inside WSL2, and I'm using Windows 11 24H2 26100.6899

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Upstream only supports the latest version so please test with 5.6. The code in question has been significantly reworked since so I would assume the bug was fixed.

Also I doubt your reproducer is complete, are you saying it panic without having any images pulled because that makes no sense as the code in question cannot be reached if there are no images. And it also contradicts podman info output which shows that you have 6 images present. So most likely one of them is causing the bug I would guess.

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
